dataset: FAA IFR Low Altitude Enroute Charts

pi_items.verified_value: IFR Low (IFR Enroute Low Altitude Chart)
pi_items.source: FAA aeronav products, Avare app documentation on GitHub shows support for 'IFR lo/hi enroute' charts

pi_items.verified_value: San Francisco (covered by FAA IFR Low Altitude Enroute Chart L-2)
pi_items.source: FAA Product Catalog lists San Francisco Low Altitude Enroute Chart L-2 as official product; search results confirm San Francisco area coverage

pi_items.verified_value: Not verified as authoritative but consistent with task artifact naming patterns
pi_items.source: Task setup scripts define this path but no external authoritative source confirms this specific path format

F-Droid Avare package listing and Google Groups discussions about Avare storage indicate standard Android scoped storage path Android/data/<package-name>. Alternative internal path is /data/data/com.ds.avare/files/tiles/

pi_items.verified_value: IFR charts are described as 'four-color chart series' but explicit background color not confirmed
pi_items.source: FAA documentation mentions 'four-color chart series' but does not explicitly state 'white' background in search results

pi_items.metadata_value: blue lines for Victor airways (from task description)
pi_items.verified_value: Victor airways are BLACK on IFR Low-Altitude Enroute charts; blue lines represent RNAV routes (T-routes)
pi_items.source: Multiple sources confirm: 'VOR airways are depicted in BLACK and identified by V followed by route number' (e.g., V107). RNAV routes are depicted in BLUE. This contradicts the task description claim that Victor airways are blue.

pi_items.verified_value: Avare is a real free EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) for Android by Apps4Av, package name com.ds.avare
pi_items.source: Official Avare website apps4av.com, F-Droid repository, GitHub repository (apps4av/avare), and multiple aviation sites confirm Avare as legitimate FAA chart EFB

pi_items.metadata_value: FAA-provided IFR Low charts available in Avare (implicit)
pi_items.verified_value: FAA IFR Low Altitude Enroute Charts are official FAA products revised every 56 days and integrated into Avare as downloadable databases
pi_items.source: FAA Aeronav Products Catalog and Avare documentation confirm Avare downloads official FAA charts

privileged_info_summary: Avare is a legitimate free EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) that displays FAA IFR Low Altitude Enroute Charts for real aviation navigation. The San Francisco IFR Low chart (L-2) is a real FAA product revised every 56 days. The standard storage path for Avare data is /sdcard/Android/data/com.ds.avare (not /sdcard/com.ds.avare as claimed in metadata). On IFR Low charts, Victor airways are depicted in BLACK lines (not blue as described in the task), while blue lines represent RNAV routes (T-routes). The charts use a four-color design; the explicit background color is not officially specified but charts are typically light-colored.
